D1 OpticsL3 ApplyReference

Gaussian Beam Waist — TEM₀₀ Mode Evolution

Laser TEM₀₀ beam: waist w₀, Rayleigh range z_R = πw₀²/λ; beam expands as w(z) = w₀√(1+(z/z_R)²), far-field divergence θ = λ/(πw₀)

A tighter focus (smaller w₀) gives shorter Rayleigh range and faster divergence — the diffraction-limit trade-off. Used in laser cutting, optical tweezers, fibre coupling. Beam-parameter product M² ≥ 1 quantifies how close a real beam is to ideal TEM₀₀.

Slide waist w₀ at 633 nm.
